Internal Legal & Compliance Memo — Aurora Lattice plc (UK issuer) — FCA/MAR oversight
Prepared by Ms. Eliza Beaumont, General Counsel; contact eliza.beaumont@auroralattice.co.uk; for Board and Compliance Committee
Subject: Non-public cyber incident and cryptoasset custody launch; ESG disclosure adjustments; insider controls and closed period
On 29 May, a targeted breach was detected in our London data centre affecting the beta environment of Prism Wallet (cryptoasset custody), with approximately 3% of test accounts experiencing unauthorised API calls; this has not been disclosed via RNS.
Preliminary internal forensics indicate credential stuffing via a supplier VPN; remediation is underway and the service launch timetable will require Board sign-off; potential delay to Q3 revenue from Prism Wallet is considered material.
Separately, the Sustainability team advises that Scope 2 figures for FY2025 require restatement due to metering errors at the Hull facility, increasing reported emissions by 14%, which may affect eligibility under our green financing covenants.
We are negotiating a revised master services agreement with Caldera Fintech Ltd, including a volume-linked fee holiday and a termination fee waiver contingent on successful cyber remediation; these commercial terms are non-public.
An insider list has been created for the above matters; initial insiders: Ms. Eliza Beaumont, Mr. Daniel Kwan, Dr. Freya March, and Mr. Owen Ridley; entries to include role, access time, and personal contacts in accordance with UK MAR.
Deal team members related to the crypto custody rollout are placed on the Restricted List and must operate behind the information barrier; Research and Sales are not to be wall-crossed without Compliance approval.
Directors and PDMRs are reminded we are within a closed period pending the Q2 trading update; no dealings, no tipping, and no selective disclosure to counterparties or suppliers.
The Board will consider whether an impairment charge is required for capitalised development costs of Prism Wallet if launch slips beyond September, which may affect guidance.
Marketing may continue generic educational content on cyber hygiene and ESG best practice for clients, provided it references public materials and avoids any incident specifics or commercial terms.
Internal training this quarter includes an overview of crypto custody risk controls and insider list maintenance; this module is operational and not linked to any transaction.
Please direct any queries to Compliance Officer Mr. Rory Lennox at rory.lennox@auroralattice.co.uk or the FCA liaison inbox fcaliaison@auroralattice.co.uk.
If a regulatory disclosure becomes necessary, Legal will prepare a draft RNS and coordinate with the FCA Primary Market Monitoring team; until then, retain documents in the “Restricted — Cyber/ESG” drive with audit logging.
